content_markdown:
A gas undergoes one cycle of a cyclic process.

The net change in internal energy of the gas is

options.label: D
options.text: determined by the initial temperature of the gas.

vibe_explanation: Internal energy is a state function, meaning its value depends only on the current state of the system (temperature, pressure, volume). In a cyclic process, the system returns to its exact initial state, so the final internal energy equals the initial internal energy, making the net change $\Delta U = 0$.
